title: Cyrtanthera aurea
intro: Cyrtanthera aurea is a plant species belonging to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the Golden Cyrtanthera. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and preventing infections. Bioactive constituents of Cyrtanthera aurea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cyrtanthera aurea is traditionally used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Cyrtanthera aurea is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which includes the majority of orchid species. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Cyrtanthera aurea likely belonging to the subfamily Orchidoideae. The subfamily Orchidoideae is characterized by its unique flowering structure and diverse range of species. Cyrtanthera aurea is likely a part of the tribe Orchideae, which comprises some of the most primitive orchids. The exact classification of Cyrtanthera aurea may require further research for a more detailed answer.
